extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won the Toulouse sprint by closing down GROENEWEGEN Dylan's leadout jump in the final 150 meters, maintaining the trend of 11 different stage winners in 11 stages.
tactical_patterns: GROENEWEGEN Dylan's team executed perfect leadout but launched sprint too early from 200m, allowing closer-positioned EWAN to time jump perfectly.
VIVIANI Elia wasted MOHORIČ and RICHEZE's leadout by repeatedly checking over shoulder instead of committing, losing momentum in final 300m.
GREIPEL André positioned far back with 5km remaining on wide roads, revealing either positioning weakness or team support deficit.
Teams surrendered peloton control under 3km despite narrow technical roads, allowing late positioning chaos that favored patient sprinters over early commits.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: demonstrated superior sprint timing and composure, waiting behind GROENEWEGEN's leadout rather than panicking when rival launched early
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: misjudged sprint distance on fast finishing straight, excellent leadout execution but premature acceleration from 200m cost certain victory
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: indecisive in final sprint despite perfect MOHORIČ-RICHEZE leadout, lost momentum checking positioning instead of committing to launch
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: positioned well on VIVIANI's wheel but lacked finishing speed for top result, tactical positioning sound but legs insufficient
